enclosed_documents: Forensic Causation Report with annotated photography
Supplement report with line-by-line variance analysis
summary_paragraphs: Please find enclosed the complete forensic documentation package for the property at 614 E 26th St. Our independent inspection has documented storm-related damage to the Roofing.

The enclosed reports include annotated photography of all identified damage, an Xactimate-format replacement cost estimate, and a forensic causation analysis establishing the relationship between documented weather events and observed damage.
Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require any additional information or wish to schedule a joint inspection.

code_violations.requirement: Drip edge required at eaves and rake edges of shingle roofs; min 2-inch overlap at joints, min 1/4-inch projection below sheathing
Underlayment shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — ASTM D226 / D4869 / D6757 labeling required
Ice barrier required in areas where the average daily temperature in January is ≤25°F or where ice damming has a history; min 2 feet inside exterior wall line (Climate Zones 5A+ including OH/NY)
Flashings shall be installed at wall and roof intersections, wherever there is a change in roof slope or direction, and around roof openings

conclusion_paragraphs: Based on our forensic analysis of the property at 614 E 26th St, the modified bitumen / flat roof system sustained confirmed storm damage consistent with the reported storm event of April 25, 2026. While detailed individual findings are pending full documentation, the forensic inspection methodology — including visual assessment, tactile evaluation, and systematic documentation of the roof membrane and associated components — was conducted in accordance with industry-standard protocols to establish the causal relationship between the storm event and the observed damage conditions.

The confirmed damage to the modified bitumen membrane and associated roofing components requires full system replacement rather than spot repair. Modified bitumen roofing systems function as a monolithic waterproofing assembly; once the membrane integrity is compromised by storm-related impacts or mechanical damage, localized patching does not restore the system to its pre-loss watertight condition and does not meet manufacturer specifications for long-term performance. Additionally, partial replacement of a modified bitumen system introduces seam vulnerabilities and material incompatibilities that fall short of the standards established under the IRC as adopted in Indiana for maintaining a continuous, code-compliant roof covering.

Based on the documented damage, applicable building codes under the IRC as adopted in the State of Indiana, and recognized industry standards, our analysis identifies the need for full replacement of the modified bitumen roof system, including membrane, flashing details, and any compromised substrate or insulation components. The forensic evidence supports a comprehensive scope that restores the roof assembly to its pre-loss condition and ensures compliance with current code requirements. As no carrier scope has been received to date, this recommendation is based solely on the physical evidence observed and documented at the property.

This determination is consistent with the professional standards set forth by HAAG Engineering certification protocols for roof damage assessment, NRCA guidelines for modified bitumen roof system evaluation and replacement criteria, and the International Residential Code (IRC) as adopted in Indiana governing roof covering integrity and replacement thresholds. These industry-recognized authorities collectively support the conclusion that storm-compromised modified bitumen systems require full replacement to ensure proper performance and code compliance.
forensic_findings.damage_summary: Supplement-only claim — see Xactimate estimate and supplement letter for line-item-level documentation.

damage_thresholds.source: NOAA SWDI + IBHS Sub-Severe Hail Study (2025)

differentiation_table.characteristics: Uniform granule loss, curling at edges, consistent deterioration pattern
differentiation_table.observed: No — damage pattern is random/localized, inconsistent with uniform aging

differentiation_table.characteristics: Systematic pattern across same production batch, uniform failure mode
differentiation_table.observed: No — damage is random and impact-related, not systematic

executive_summary: On April 25, 2026, the property located at 614 E 26th St, IN, sustained storm damage affecting its modified bitumen / flat roof membrane system and associated building envelope components. A forensic inspection was conducted to document the condition of the roofing assembly, flashing details, drainage infrastructure, and ancillary building elements in relation to the reported storm event. The roof system consists of a modified bitumen membrane application, a material particularly susceptible to impact damage and wind-driven compromise at seams, laps, and perimeter terminations.

Field examination of the modified bitumen membrane revealed impact-related damage consistent with storm activity, including puncture points, granule displacement, and compromised lap seams across the roof field. Perimeter flashing and edge metal details exhibited deformation and lifting consistent with wind loading forces generated during the April 25, 2026 event. These findings indicate that the storm event produced sufficient force to breach the weatherproofing integrity of the flat roof assembly, creating active or incipient moisture intrusion pathways that compromise the substrate and interior building envelope.

The documented damage to the modified bitumen roof system necessitates full replacement rather than spot repair due to the inherent limitations of partial membrane restoration on an aged assembly. Sectional patching of a modified bitumen system cannot restore uniform adhesion, consistent granule coverage, or watertight lap integrity to meet the performance standards referenced under the IRC, and localized repairs to a weathered membrane introduce differential expansion zones that accelerate future failure. Additionally, any roof repair or replacement activity that exceeds the applicable threshold under IRC Section R908.3.1.1 triggers code compliance requirements for the full assembly, including underlayment and fastening specifications, which cannot be achieved through piecemeal remediation of the existing system.

code_citation.jurisdiction: Indiana Residential Code (IRC 2018 with state amendments)
code_citation.requirement: Underlayment required over entire roof deck area not covered by ice barrier
code_citation.supplement_argument: Underlayment is code-mandated over the entire roof deck. Areas where ice & water barrier is not installed require felt or synthetic underlayment. Omission does not meet code requirements.

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Required at eaves, valleys, and around penetrations per GAF system requirements
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: GAF warranty VOID without WeatherWatch/StormGuard at eaves and valleys

code_citation.requirement: Ice barrier required at eaves extending from eave edge to at least 24 inches past interior wall line, and in valleys
code_citation.supplement_argument: Ice & water barrier is required by code at all eaves and valleys. This is not an optional upgrade — it is a mandatory building code requirement for a code-compliant installation.
code_citation.title: Ice Barrier Requirement

line_items.description: Ice & water barrier (2 courses eaves + 1 course valleys)
line_items.supplement_argument: Ice & water barrier is required by code at all eaves and valleys. This is not an optional upgrade — it is a mandatory building code requirement for a code-compliant installation.

code_citation.jurisdiction: Indiana Residential Code (IRC 2018 with state amendments)
code_citation.requirement: Drip edge shall be provided at eaves and gable rake edges of shingle roofs
code_citation.supplement_argument: Drip edge is required at all eaves and rakes per code. Protects fascia and deck edge from water damage. Not an optional upgrade.
code_citation.title: Drip Edge Requirement
